body.objective: Add a runtime contract harness so Codex/OpenCode worker adapters are probed and validated before real worker execution.
constraints: Do not spawn a real worker when adapter contract is drifted or unavailable.
Keep Codex/OpenCode as runtime adapters; do not add Ruflo runtime dependency.
Do not store secrets or bearer tokens in probe output.
acceptance_criteria: Codex probe proves current CLI supports exec --json --cd before execution.
OpenCode probe proves current CLI supports run --format json --dir before execution.
Drifted fake runtime blocks execute-worker before spawn and records an actionable gate.
Runtime contract status is exposed through API and dashboard-readable metadata.

body.objective: Reduce token burn for small closed-loop workers using a low-context worker profile and token efficiency metrics.
constraints: Do not hide runtime usage; parse and report real usage.
Small low-risk doc fixes must have conservative default token ceilings.
Any profile change that weakens policy or security boundaries requires review.
acceptance_criteria: Worker lease metadata records tokens_per_diff_line and tokens_per_successful_worker.
A low-risk doc loop can enforce max_tokens_per_worker from real runtime usage.
Dashboard exposes token efficiency without raw stdout inspection.
Over-budget worker execution blocks additional leases.

body.objective: Add an independent checker worker execution bridge that reviews maker output through a read-only lease and writes verdict evidence.
constraints: Checker must not mutate files.
Checker cannot be the same lease as the maker.
Checker output is advisory until deterministic gates also pass.
High-risk scopes still require security checker or human gate.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/loops/runs/:id/execute-checker executes a prepared checker lease.
Checker receives maker diff, assignment packet, stdout/stderr and deterministic check evidence.
Checker stdout/stderr, runtime usage, checkpoints and trace spans are persisted.
Accepted checker verdict moves a run toward verification; rejected verdict blocks or requests revision.

body.objective: Move worker control artifacts into an ignored .djimitflo control directory so worktree status and diff gates show only patch output.
constraints: Historical assignment packets must remain readable.
Review bundles must still expose work instruction and packet paths.
Control artifact movement must not hide real source changes.
acceptance_criteria: Prepared maker worktree has no untracked LOOP_WORK.md or ASSIGNMENT_PACKET.json at repo root.
Control files live under .djimitflo/ and are ignored in worker git status.
Diff threshold gates count patch files only.
Review bundle still links assignment packet and work instructions.

body.objective: Parse runtime stdout/stderr warnings into structured lease metadata, gates and dashboard telemetry.
constraints: Warnings may not leak secrets.
Low-risk warning handling should be advisory by default.
High-risk trust-boundary warnings must block completion.
acceptance_criteria: Plugin hook parse warnings are captured as runtime_warnings.
Skill context budget warnings are captured as runtime_warnings.
Warning classes are visible in review bundle and dashboard.
High-risk warning policy can block completion without blocking low-risk advisory runs.

body.objective: Close the worker loop by automatically running deterministic checks and checker/security gates before marking output ready for human merge.
constraints: Maker success alone cannot complete mutating work.
Do not merge, push or deploy automatically.
High-risk scopes require security checker pass.
Completion must preserve stdout/stderr, trace, checkpoint and gate evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Loop status supports ready_for_human_merge.
Maker completion triggers deterministic checks before checker pass is accepted.
Checker pass plus deterministic gates moves mutating work to ready_for_human_merge.
High-risk runs remain blocked without security checker verdict.
End-to-end live smoke proves maker, checker, gates, traces, checkpoints, artifacts and real token usage.

body.objective: Expose runtime contract status, warning gates, token efficiency and maker/checker actions in the dashboard operator flow.
constraints: Dashboard must not expose secrets or bearer tokens.
Dashboard actions must call the same guarded API routes as CLI automation.
Dashboard must distinguish prepared, running, verifying and ready_for_human_merge.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shows runtime contract cards for Codex and OpenCode.
Dashboard shows runtime warning count and token efficiency per worker.
Dashboard can run prepared maker and checker leases without bypassing gates.
Dashboard build passes.

body.objective: Run the final end-to-end real worker smoke on temp DB/temp repo proving maker, checker, deterministic gates, traces, checkpoints, artifacts and budget handling.
constraints: Use temp DB and temp repository for smoke.
Do not touch production/default Djimitflo database.
Do not inspect or print secrets.
Stop temporary servers and prove no listener remains.
acceptance_criteria: Live Codex maker smoke exits zero and stores artifacts under .data/agent-evidence.
Live checker smoke exits zero or records actionable runtime contract failure.
Runtime token usage is parsed from real runtime output and enforced by token gate.
Trace spans and before/after checkpoints exist for maker and checker.
Temporary server is stopped and port is free after smoke.

body.objective: Add a visible worker pool and queue model for Codex/OpenCode fleets so Djimitflo can scale beyond one active worker while preserving leases, budgets and worktree isolation.
constraints: Worker pool scaling must be bounded by CPU, memory, runtime availability, token budgets and repository worktree limits.
Prepared leases, queued leases and running leases must be counted separately.
No automatic worker spawn without operator approval or an explicit scheduler policy.
acceptance_criteria: API exposes queue_depth, prepared_leases, running_leases, available_capacity and blocked_capacity_reasons per runtime.
Scheduler can select the next executable lease without exceeding configured concurrency limits.
Worker pool metrics distinguish Codex, OpenCode, mock and manual leases.
Dashboard shows worker pool capacity and queue pressure at a glance.

body.objective: Make the full fleet state visible in the dashboard: topology, queue, throughput, budgets, bottlenecks, warnings, artifacts and next safe actions.
constraints: Dashboard must show operational truth, not planned architecture only.
Dashboard must not expose secrets or raw bearer tokens.
Every displayed scale claim must be backed by API data, runtime evidence or explicit unknown state.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shows fleet topology from goal -> loop -> lease -> runtime -> artifact -> gate.
Dashboard shows throughput metrics: completed workers, failed workers, average runtime, token burn and tokens per useful diff.
Dashboard shows bottlenecks: no runtime, budget exhausted, blocked gates, missing checker, missing human approval.
Dashboard offers explicit next safe actions for prepared maker/checker leases.

body.objective: Use workstation resource signals to determine safe worker concurrency and prevent overload while maximizing useful throughput.
constraints: Concurrency must degrade when free memory, CPU load, runtime availability or token budget is constrained.
The MacBook dashboard may observe and control; the workstation remains the execution node.
Resource-aware scheduling must be explainable in dashboard and API responses.
acceptance_criteria: API exposes recommended_concurrency by runtime with reasoned capacity limits.
Scheduler refuses new running workers when resource thresholds are exceeded and records why.
Dashboard shows workstation capacity, current load, recommended concurrency and blocked scaling reasons.
A smoke test can simulate low capacity and prove new workers are not started.

body.objective: Connect backlog, goals, loops and worker pools into a controlled scale execution flow that can process many bounded work items with evidence and review gates.
constraints: Backlog projection can create goals and prepared leases, but running workers requires capacity and policy approval.
Each work item must preserve source reference, value score, risk class and evidence trail.
Completion must update backlog status without hiding failed or rejected worker output.
acceptance_criteria: Triaged backlog items can be converted into goals, loop runs and prepared maker/checker leases in batches.
Fleet execution can process multiple independent low-risk items in separate worktrees under concurrency limits.
Dashboard shows backlog-to-worker progress and preserves failed/rejected artifacts.
End-to-end smoke proves at least two prepared worker paths can be visible and schedulable without file conflicts.
